original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: United States Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021 — Genital Ulcers, Anogenital Warts, and Human Papillomavirus Infections
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United States federal public-health guidance requiring UAE epidemiology, laboratory, medicine and reporting adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Sexually active adolescents and adults with genital, anal or perianal ulcers, anogenital warts, HPV infection or related counselling needs, with pregnancy, immune-status, anatomy and exposure qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Clinical STI evaluation, diagnosis discussion, counselling and clinician-selected management.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for recording lesion history and examination, differential context, clinician-selected testing, entered wart or HPV findings, partner or STI context and counselling. It does not diagnose an STI, infer sexual exposure, choose treatment or define UAE reporting pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Diseases characterized by genital, anal, or perianal ulcers — diagnostic considerations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports history, examination, ulcer site and number, lymph-node, systemic, pregnancy, HIV and clinician-selected syphilis, herpes or other evaluation context while stating that history and examination alone may be inaccurate.

exact_sections.heading: Anogenital warts — diagnostic considerations and counselling
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-observed wart morphology and site, differential diagnosis, biopsy context when indicated, immune and pregnancy context, and counselling without inferring HPV type or cancer.

exact_sections.heading: Human papillomavirus infections — prevention and counselling
exact_sections.locator: official HPV page prevention and counselling sections
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting an entered HPV result, the limitations of source attribution and timing, partner discussion and screening-programme context; it does not diagnose cancer or identify the source of infection.

title: Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021 — Genital Ulcers, Anogenital Warts, and Human Papillomavirus Infections
publisher: United States Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
jurisdiction: United States federal public-health guidance requiring UAE epidemiology, laboratory, medicine and reporting adaptation
population: Sexually active adolescents and adults with genital, anal or perianal ulcers, anogenital warts, HPV infection or related counselling needs, with pregnancy, immune-status, anatomy and exposure qualifiers.
intended_setting: Clinical STI evaluation, diagnosis discussion, counselling and clinician-selected management.
